summary: Iteration 4: Validated the new admin user management endpoints + UI and the public footer Admin Login link. Created /app/backend/tests/test_admin_users.py (14 pytest tests, all PASS) covering GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE for /api/admin/users incl. auth, duplicate (409), short pw (400), self-delete block (400), and full create→login→reset-pw→login-fails-old→login-succeeds-new→delete→login-401 lifecycle. Frontend Playwright run on the public preview URL verified: footer-admin-login link (text 'Admin Login' → /admin/login), sidebar admin-nav-admin-users routes to /admin/users, add-admin-form creates a new admin with toast + row added without reload, duplicate email shows 'already exists' toast, reset-pw modal opens via reset-pw-<id>, accepts new password and closes on save, self delete-admin-<my-id> button is disabled, deleting another admin removes the row, and primary admin login still works. All TEST_ / ui_test_ admins cleaned up — only the seeded devanshpandey8933@gmail.com remains.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py POST /api/admin/users correctly hashes with bcrypt, lowercases+strips the email, enforces password>=6, returns 409 on duplicate, and excludes password_hash/_id in responses. Good.
server.py DELETE /api/admin/users/{id} guards both self-deletion (400) and the last-admin case (400) BEFORE issuing the delete — correct ordering.
GET /api/admin/users projects out password_hash and _id — no sensitive/non-serializable leakage. Hard-coded list cap is to_list(100); fine for small admin teams but document if you ever expect more.

Frontend AdminUsersPage.jsx calls fetchUsers() after add/delete which is correct; reset-password handler optimistically closes the modal but does NOT call fetchUsers — currently fine because the table doesn't surface password state, but if you add 'last reset' columns later, refresh the list.
AdminLayout sidebar adds 'Admin Users' to NAV with end={false} — active state will correctly highlight only at /admin/users, OK.

Footer 'Admin Login' link uses react-router Link (client-side nav). Good. Renders inside the footer-bottom row beside the agency tagline. Contrast is acceptable on the dark footer (white/45 base, hover EAA015).
Backend still uses deprecated @app.on_event hooks — not blocking, same finding as iteration 3.

success_rate.backend: 100% (14/14 new pytest in test_admin_users.py; iteration_3 32/32 still green)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (footer link, nav, create, duplicate-error, reset-pw, self-delete disabled, delete-other all pass)

test_credentials: Primary admin: devanshpandey8933@gmail.com / nepaltrip123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). JWT stored in localStorage as nepaltrip_admin_jwt.
seed_data_creation: Several short-lived admin users were created during testing (ui_test_*, test_create_*, test_dup_*, test_login_*, test_pw_*, test_del_*, verify_*) — ALL cleaned up via DELETE /api/admin/users/{id} at the end. Only the seeded primary admin remains in db.users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: For admin-user-management regression run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://adventure-nepal-hub.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_admin_users.py -v. Key UI testids: footer-admin-login, admin-nav-admin-users, admin-users-page, add-admin-form, new-admin-email, new-admin-name, new-admin-password, add-admin-submit, admin-user-row-<id>, reset-pw-<id>, reset-pw-modal, reset-pw-input, reset-pw-submit, delete-admin-<id> (disabled for the current user). Note: When verifying the new admin's login from an outside Python script, use the requests library — raw urllib gets a 403 from the preview proxy due to UA filtering; this is NOT an app issue.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. New feature works end-to-end.
